text: Lesson 53
zh: 第53课

text: In the public interest
zh: 为了公众利益

text: What could not be reported in the official files?
zh: 官方档案中无法记载的是什么？

text: The Scandinavian countries are much admired all over the world for their enlightened social policies.
zh: 斯堪的纳维亚国家因其开明的社会政策而受到全世界的推崇。

text: Sweden has evolved an excellent system for protecting the individual citizen from high-handed or incompetent public officers.
zh: 瑞典发展出一套出色的制度，用以保护公民个人免受专横或无能官员的侵害。

text: The system has worked so well, that it has been adopted in other countries too.
zh: 这套制度运行得如此成功，以至于其他国家也纷纷采用。

text: The Swedes were the first to recognize that public officials like civil servants,
zh: 瑞典人首先认识到，像公务员、

text: police officers, health inspectors or tax-collectors can make mistakes or act over-zealously in the belief that they are serving the public.
zh: 警察、卫生检查员或税务人员这样的公职人员，可能会犯错误，或者出于自认为在为公众服务的信念而行事过于热心。

text: As long ago as 1809, the Swedish Parliament introduced a scheme to safeguard the interest of the individual.
zh: 早在1809年，瑞典议会就推出了一项旨在保护公民个人利益的方案。

text: A parliamentary committee representing all political parties appoints a person who is suitably qualified to investigate private grievances against the State.
zh: 由各政党代表组成的议会委员会，会任命一位合适的人选，负责调查民众对国家的申诉。

text: The official title of the person is 'Justiteombudsman', but the Swedes commonly refer to him as the 'J.O.' or 'Ombudsman'.
zh: 这一职位的正式名称是“司法特派员”（Justiteombudsman），但瑞典人通常称他为“J.O.”或“申诉专员”（Ombudsman）。

text: The Ombudsman is not subject to political pressure.
zh: 申诉专员不受政治压力的左右。

text: He investigates complaints large and small that come to him from all levels of society.
zh: 他调查来自社会各阶层的大大小小的投诉。

text: As complaints must be made in writing, the Ombudsman receives an average of 1, 200 letters a year.
zh: 由于投诉必须以书面形式提出，申诉专员平均每年收到1200封信。

text: He has eight lawyer assistants to help him and he examines every single letter in detail.
zh: 他有八名律师助理协助工作，他会详细审阅每一封信。

text: There is nothing secretive about the Ombudsman's work, for his correspondence is open to public inspection.
zh: 申诉专员的工作并无任何秘密可言，因为他的往来信函都公开供公众查阅。

text: If a citizen's complaint is justified the Ombudsman will act on his behalf.
zh: 如果公民的投诉合理，申诉专员就会代表他采取行动。

text: The action he takes varies according to the nature of the complaint.
zh: 他所采取的行动会根据投诉的性质而有所不同。

text: He may gently reprimand an official or even suggest to parliament that a law be altered.
zh: 他可能会温和地训诫某位官员，甚至向议会建议修改某项法律。

text: The following case is a typical example of the Ombudsman's work.
zh: 以下这个案例就是申诉专员工作的一个典型例子。

text: A foreigner living in a Swedish village wrote to the Ombudsman complaining that he had been ill-treated by the police,
zh: 一位住在瑞典乡村的外国人写信给申诉专员，投诉自己受到警察的虐待，

text: simply because he was a foreigner.
zh: 原因仅仅是因为他是外国人。

text: The Ombudsman immediately wrote to the Chief of Police in the district asking him to send a record of the case.
zh: 申诉专员立即写信给该地区的警察局长，要求他提供此案的记录。

text: There was nothing in the record to show that the foreigner's complaint was justified
zh: 记录中并没有任何内容能证明这名外国人的投诉属实，

text: and the Chief of Police strongly denied the accusation.
zh: 警察局长也极力否认这一指控。

text: It was impossible for the Ombudsman to take action,
zh: 申诉专员无法采取任何行动，

text: but when he received a similar complaint from another foreigner in the same village,
zh: 但当他收到同一村庄另一名外国人提出的类似投诉时，

text: he immediately sent one of his lawyers to investigate the matter.
zh: 他立即派了一名律师前去调查此事。

text: The lawyer ascertained that a policeman had indeed dealt roughly with foreigners on several occasions.
zh: 这名律师查明，确实有一名警察多次粗暴对待外国人。

text: The fact that the policeman was prejudiced against foreigners could not be recorded in the official files.
zh: 这名警察对外国人怀有偏见这一事实，是无法记录在官方档案中的。

text: It was only possible for the Ombudsman to find this out by sending one of his representatives to check the facts.
zh: 申诉专员只有通过派代表实地核查事实，才有可能查明这一点。

text: The policeman in question was severely reprimanded
zh: 涉事的这名警察受到了严厉训诫，

text: and was informed that if any further complaints were lodged against him, he would be prosecuted.
zh: 并被告知，如果再有人对他提出投诉，他将被起诉。

text: The Ombudsman's prompt action at once put an end to an unpleasant practice which might have gone unnoticed.
zh: 申诉专员的及时行动，立刻终止了一种本可能不为人知、继续存在的令人不快的行为。
